extraction.one_line_summary: ASGREEN won from a 185km breakaway with ABRAHAMSEN and CAMPENAERTS, holding off the peloton by approximately 10 meters after never gaining more than 90 seconds advantage.
tactical_patterns: ASGREEN, ABRAHAMSEN, CAMPENAERTS survived 185km breakaway with only 90-second max gap, peloton misjudged catch timing by mere meters.
Soudal prioritized stage hunting over sprint train support, deploying ASGREEN in ultra-long breakaway despite marginal gap throughout.
Peloton's chase calculation catastrophically failed on flat terrain despite maintaining visual contact, suggesting tempo miscalibration rather than strength deficit.

rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: positive: First Tour stage win after years off form, showed tactical patience holding sprint until final meters against breakaway companions
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: negative: Best sprinter finish but failed fifth stage win despite perfect team positioning, caught 10 meters too late
rider_signals.ABRAHAMSEN Jonas: positive: Tour debutant contested finish strongly against experienced ASGREEN despite losing sprint, indicates Tour-level maturity
rider_signals.GESCHKE Simon: negative: Nearly last overall, described as miserable on mountain stages, struggling to finish within time limits
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: neutral: Controlled yellow jersey effortlessly with 7:35 advantage, team allowed breakaway freedom without chase contribution
